title: TransportInner bundles C context/config/vtable/CString in one Box — defensible against splitting into separate ClientInner fields
description: TcpInner/UdsInner/ShmInner each heap-allocate the transport_ctx, transport_cfg, transport_cb, and the CString that backs the pointer field in cfg — all together in one Box. This keeps all FFI pointer targets stable in a single allocation and ensures they are always co-located. A contributor might suggest flattening these into separate fields on ClientInner (or the top-level Client) to 'simplify.' That would break the address-stability invariant because ClientInner is already inside a Pin<Box>, but the individual CStrings and config structs need their own stable addresses independently — the C transport_cfg holds a raw pointer into the CString, not into ClientInner. The bundled-Box design is the correct layout.
